title: Juliet Watts
content:
People.ContactEmail jwatts@mccarthy.ca
- Dalhousie University
Juliet obtained her Juris Doctor and Certificate in Law and Technology from the Schulich School of Law at Dalhousie University in 2025. She previously earned her Bachelor of Arts in Political Science and Public Administration from the University of Victoria.
Throughout her academic career, Juliet was actively engaged in university governance and student leadership. At the University of Victoria, she served as an undergraduate member of the Board of Governors and Senate, as well as a Director of the University of Victoria Students’ Society. At Dalhousie, Juliet was President of the Law Students’ Society and Co-Director of the Pro Bono Program.
In her final year of law school, Juliet was a finalist and factum prize recipient in the Harold G. Fox Intellectual Property Moot. Upon graduating, she received the Horace E. Read Award for the greatest overall contribution to scholarship and student life.
